commits.title: Retire ggen-core in favor of a first-principles engine
docs: track JTBD-verification bugs in-repo + ggen-core removal proposal
docs+examples: archive stale ggen-core-era content, fix live CLI syntax
remove ggen-core, rewrite README from first principles
Prepare crates.io publish readiness for the 7 real publish targets
docs(aps): add machine-readable claims/standing ledger per APS 2030 audit
docs: fix systemic drift found by a 10-agent Rust-core-team documentation audit
fix(publish): mark cpmp, genesis-types-v2, genesis-core-v2 publish=false
fix: disable broken commands, fix policy/doctor/init bugs, archive examples, add canonical clap-noun-verb-cli example
feat(examples): receiptctl — multi-pack combined CLI (3 real projects)

changes.entity: crates/ggen-core (whole crate)

evidence.note: git rm, directory absent from disk
removed from [workspace] members, moved to exclude first

changes.mitigation: ggen-engine + praxis-core + praxis-graphlaw (vendored from ~/praxis) replace it as the live pipeline

changes.entity: Stage-0 schema dispatch (schema_dispatch.rs, config_schema.rs)
changes.detail: Single shared classify-then-parse point (ConfigSchemaClassification -> ParsedGgenToml) replaces 6 ad-hoc call sites (sync, handle_doctor, handle_graph_validate, build_law_engine) that previously each hardcoded a schema assumption. Fixes BUG-005 (doctor unconditionally called frontmatter-only GgenConfig::load).

changes.entity: ReceiptPayload#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]
changes.detail: Tampered/corrupted stored receipt payloads with extra JSON fields now fail deserialization instead of silently loading.

changes.entity: crates/ggen-cli/src/cmds/{sync,graph,template,doctor}.rs — orphaned dead code with live ggen_core imports

evidence.note: use ggen_core::codegen::{...} against a deleted crate
corresponding pub mod declarations are commented out, so this compiles despite dangling imports

changes.detail: Unlike wizard.rs/sigma.rs/inverse_sync.rs (deleted outright in PR #259), these 4 files were left on disk with real (non-commented) imports of the fully-deleted ggen-core crate. Only escapes compile failure because their `pub mod` lines are commented out in mod.rs. A maintainer grepping the cmds/ directory listing (not mod.rs) would wrongly assume these are live.

changes.entity: examples/receiptctl (multi-pack CLI) + shared pack ontology dedup fix

evidence.note: unions 6 packs from 3 upstream projects via real [packs] mechanism
duplicate pi:wasmExport "discover_dfg" renamed to unique discover_optimized_dfg
duplicate export renamed to discover_streaming_log

changes.detail: Fixed a real duplicate-data bug (2 different packs asserting the same wasmExport name) that only surfaced when combining packs — caused duplicate SPARQL rows -> duplicate Rust enum variants -> compile error. Fix is at the shared-pack level, benefits all downstream consumers.

changes.entity: CLI commands removed: wizard, sigma, inverse_sync

evidence.note: -1744 lines, deleted outright
-49 lines
-359 lines

changes.detail: All three were already experimental/feature-gated or disconnected before removal — no default-build regression. Users invoking these now get clap's 'unrecognized subcommand'.

changes.entity: inject_default_verbs() + linkme force-link shim
changes.detail: Rewrites bare `ggen sync`->`ggen sync run`, `ggen doctor`->`ggen doctor run`, `ggen receipt`->`ggen receipt verify` before dispatch to clap_noun_verb::CommandRegistry. `use ggen_engine as _;` force-links ggen-engine's #[linkme::distributed_slice] noun/verb registrations into the binary. `graph` deliberately excluded (no single obvious default verb).

changes.entity: ggen agent install / ggen capability inspect — disabled with typed refusal

evidence.note: BUG-004; real logic preserved as install_impl, #[allow(dead_code)]
GAP-001; real logic preserved as inspect_impl

changes.detail: Both commands now always Err(NounVerbError::execution_error(...)) regardless of arguments — deliberate fail-closed design (a functioning-looking command became a hard dead end) rather than silently-wrong behavior. Still registered in --help, so automation depending on prior success paths breaks with a clear error, not silently.

changes.entity: Workspace membership churn (+7 crates, ggen-core removed)

evidence.note: added ggen-engine, praxis-core, praxis-graphlaw, powl2-decompose, chicago-tdd-tools, bcinr-pddl, bcinr-mfw-ir; removed ggen-core
added ggen-cheat-scanner (PR #257)

changes.detail: Root ggen package's [[bin]] removed, autobins=false — root becomes library-only; canonical binary now built from ggen-cli-lib.

changes.entity: Publish-flag narrowing: 7 -> 4 real crates.io targets

evidence.note: publish = false added

changes.detail: Real publish targets narrowed to ggen, ggen-config, ggen-graph, ggen-marketplace (only 4 crates with no publish= line). cargo publish on the other 3 now correctly refuses instead of silently being publishable-but-unintended.

changes.entity: ConfigSchemaClassification / ParsedGgenToml (new types)
changes.detail: 5-variant classifier (DeclarativeRules, Frontmatter, Ambiguous{matched}, Unsupported{observed_markers}, Malformed{diagnostic}) fails closed with typed codes rather than a generic TOML deserialize error.

changes.entity: GgenManifest::PackRef vs GgenConfig::PackRef — duplication remains

evidence.note: flat struct PackRef
untagged enum PackRef { Path, Git }

changes.detail: Two independently-defined, incompatible PackRef types for the same [packs] table name, unresolved by this window's changes. ConfigSchemaClassification manages the ambiguity at dispatch time rather than eliminating the duplication. Each type's doc comment cross-references the other as 'not the same type' — a documented, not hidden, gap.

changes.entity: 5 fail-open -> fail-closed bug fixes (BUG-001/002/003/004/005, GAP-001, FM-GEN-008)

evidence.note: BUG-001: malformed lockfile entry no longer aborts whole command via bare ?
BUG-002/003: --force garbage no longer silently becomes false
BUG-005: doctor now dispatches through schema classifier
FM-GEN-008: 10 typed TemplateFailureCause variants replace one generic message

changes.detail: Previously silent failures now surface correctly: init --force garbage, init error JSON payloads (were exit 0 despite status:error body), capability enable with malformed pack ids, agent install/capability inspect misleading exit-0-with-wrong-data.

changes.entity: E0011/E0013 stay warnings unless strict_mode=true

evidence.note: unchanged since #255, not touched by later commits in this window

changes.detail: By-design gap per module doc, but non-strict projects still get non-deterministic ORDER BY silently logged, not blocked.

changes.entity: New receipt-chain SLO gate (justfile slo-check, receipt_chain_e2e.rs, 180s)

evidence.note: slo-check recipe did not exist before this commit
new file, 739 lines, 15 tests

changes.detail: Not a changed baseline — this is brand-new automated timing infrastructure. No Duration/Instant assertions inside the test file itself; the 180s bound lives only in the justfile's real date-based wall-clock wrapper.

changes.entity: cli_startup_performance bench — untouched

evidence.note: zero diff across all 13 commits

changes.detail: Removing ggen-core changes the dependency graph (root package now library-only) but no static evidence the bench's compiled binary or startup path was touched; not independently re-run this session.

changes.entity: sync.rs / watch.rs pipeline is synchronous, not async

evidence.note: no tokio::spawn, no async fn, no .await anywhere
std::sync::mpsc::channel + thread::spawn (blocking OS thread, not tokio)

changes.detail: Corrects an assumption in this audit's own D7 agent prompt (which described 'async pipeline stages') — the five-stage pipeline is fully synchronous. Concurrency primitives used: Arc<dyn GraphEngine> (immutable, no Mutex), std::sync::Mutex<LawState> in graph.rs:760, static OnceLock<Mutex<()>> test lock in keys.rs:232-234.

changes.entity: Concurrency-heavy code deleted alongside ggen-core (dead, not live)

evidence.note: mock servers with tokio::spawn, Arc<RwLock<...>> caches

changes.detail: PR #259's own commit message confirms zero live Cargo dependency edges to any of this at deletion time — no new race conditions introduced, nothing incrementally modified.

changes.entity: London TDD mock removal (308 files, +1210/-14826 lines)

evidence.note: mock_a2a_server.rs (1758 lines), mock_mcp_server.rs (1477 lines), e2e_bridge_tests.rs (1501 lines) deleted
-65 lines
-1249 lines
MockRegistryClient struct + 3 tests removed, ~15 real assert_cmd tests kept

changes.detail: No tests-archive/ directory exists on disk despite CLAUDE.md prescribing archival — mock tests were git rm'd outright, not archived (only docs were archived via git mv). Net effect: fewer but more trustworthy tests.

changes.entity: ggen-cheat-scanner added (4 AST rules, wired into pre-commit)

evidence.note: 480 lines, 4 syn-based rules: CHEAT-T01 vacuous-assert, CHEAT-T02 tautological-result-check, CHEAT-T03 no-assertion-test, CHEAT-T04 mock-import
wired as guard-cheat-scan, part of the 8-gate pre-commit chain

changes.detail: Real, intentional red gate — currently fails just pre-commit, not decorative.

changes.entity: Cheat-scan finding count discrepancy: 515 (commit/jira/justfile) vs 464 (CLAUDE.md)

evidence.note: 515 findings
515 findings (comment)
~464 findings, introduced by the doc-drift-fix pass itself, not reconciled with jira/justfile

changes.detail: No scanner re-run evidence exists between the two counts in this window. Plausible explanation (515 -> 464 via ggen-core/src/* deletion in PR #259) is inference, not confirmed. cargo could not be executed in this audit's sandbox to get a live count (pinned nightly toolchain missing cargo binary despite rustup reporting it installed).

changes.entity: ggen.construct MCP tool response schema drift

evidence.note: switched from ggen_core::codegen::pipeline::GenerationPipeline to ggen_engine::sync::sync(...)

changes.detail: Dropped fields: duration_ms (no equivalent in ggen-engine's SyncReport), per-file size_bytes/content_hash. Added: graph_hash_hex. files_count now sourced from report.written.len() instead of state.generated_files.len(). No CHANGELOG or MCP schema doc found updated alongside this change. External MCP clients relying on duration_ms/content_hash break silently (missing keys, not an error).

changes.entity: ReceiptRecord / keys.rs / verbs/receipt.rs — vendored invariant chain

evidence.note: 190 lines, new. recompute_chain_hash() at L124-130, doc comment states it 'can never silently diverge from the live emission path'
369 lines, new. Ed25519 key resolution; create_new/O_CREAT|O_EXCL guarantees never-overwrite under races
18 lines, generated, routes to handlers::handle_receipt_verify/history

changes.detail: Receipt verification chain (verbs/receipt.rs -> handlers::handle_receipt_verify -> praxis-core::ReceiptRecord::recompute_chain_hash) is structurally intact post-ggen-core-removal, no ggen-core references anywhere in it. Not modified after initial vendoring; only unit-test-verified in this window, no runtime re-execution.

changes.entity: docs/aps/claims.toml ledger — admitted self-flagged drift

evidence.note: release.package-contents-clean: method field says '7 real publish targets' while its own notes field (added same commit) says the real count is now 4

changes.detail: This is an admitted, not hidden, drift — the ledger's own README.md:30-32 states evidence should only be updated when its falsifier is actually re-run. Deliberate, documented gap rather than silent staleness.

changes.entity: release.rustsec-quick-xml claim — BLOCKED, no exception_admitted_by
changes.detail: By design fails guard-publish-standing's full (non-schema-only) mode. just pre-commit's guard-claims-schema only runs --schema-only, so this BLOCKED state doesn't currently gate commits, only a real cargo publish.

gaps_summary.class: Fail-Open Behavior (BLOCKED, gated at publish only)
